# Code of Conduct

Welcome to GoSynUtils! This is a personal project of mine, but I welcome contributions, feedback, and discussions. To keep things running smoothly, here are the ground rules for interacting in this repository.

## The Golden Rule

**Keep it professional and focus on the technical stuff.** You are completely welcome to show your casual side, joke around, and be yourself. Just don't be a jerk, don't harass anyone, and keep the main focus on writing good Go code.

## What is Encouraged

* **Constructive Feedback:** I can take criticism. If my code can be faster, cleaner, or more idiomatic, point it out!
* **Casual Collaboration:** We are developers building tools, not robots. A relaxed, friendly tone is always welcome.
* **Focus on the Tech:** Keep discussions centered on the code, architecture, and the goal of zero dependencies.

## What is Not Allowed

* **Entitlement:** This is my personal repository. I am happy to review PRs and hear suggestions, but please do not act entitled to rule over the repo or demand immediate free support. I have the final say on what gets merged.
* **Harassment or Hostility:** Any form of harassment, personal attacks, or deliberate harm towards anyone in the issues or PRs will not be tolerated.

## Enforcement

I am generally very lenient. If a discussion gets too heated or someone crosses a line, my first step will almost always be to give a friendly warning to cool off.

However, in extreme cases—such as blatant harassment or severe entitlement—I reserve the right to close your issue/PR and block you from the repository without warning.

## Contact
If you need to report an extreme issue that shouldn't be public, you can reach out to me directly at **60792595+Synertry@users.noreply.github.com** or faster via Discord: [synertry](https://discordapp.com/users/134635312277225472).

*** *Note: This is a pragmatic, developer-first Code of Conduct tailored for this specific project. Let's just write some good code.*
